call_chain.name: SearchWorker::search
call_chain.note: Entry point; routes to search_reader, search_preprocessor, search_decompress, or search_path based on haystack type

call_chain.name: SearchWorker::search_reader
call_chain.note: Method wrapper; delegates to free function search_reader with matcher unpacked from PatternMatcher enum

call_chain.name: search_reader (free fn)
call_chain.note: Dispatch point: matches Printer enum, calls p.sink_with_path() to instantiate StandardSink/SummarySink/JSONSink, then calls Searcher::search_reader

call_chain.name: Searcher::search_reader
call_chain.note: Core searcher; performs byte-level search and drives Sink callbacks (begin, matched, context, finish)

change_impact_map.change: Define CSV<W>, CSVBuilder, CSVSink<'p,'s,M,W>; implement Sink for CSVSink writing matched lines as RFC-4180 CSV rows; implement has_match() and stats() accessors; implement CSV::sink_with_path() returning CSVSink
Add 'mod csv;' and re-export CSV, CSVBuilder, CSVSink in the pub use block alongside existing Standard/Summary/JSON exports
Add CSV(grep::printer::CSV<W>) variant to the Printer<W> enum
Add Printer::CSV(ref mut p) => p.get_mut() arm to the get_mut() match in impl<W: WriteColor> Printer<W>
Add CSV arm to search_path() free fn match block: instantiate CSVSink via p.sink_with_path(), call searcher.search_path(), return SearchResult
Add CSV arm to search_reader() free fn match block: instantiate CSVSink via p.sink_with_path(), call searcher.search_reader(), return SearchResult
Accept 'csv' as a valid --output-format value; in printer-construction code match on 'csv' and build Printer::CSV(...) variant
